Title: IFE System Availability
Statement: The In-Flight Entertainment system shall achieve a minimum operational availability of 99.5% across all seat units during any given flight sector.

Title: Boot Time
Statement: The IFE system shall complete its boot sequence and present the passenger welcome screen within 90 seconds of aircraft power-on.

Title: Graceful Degradation
Statement: In the event of a single Seat Display Unit failure, the IFE system shall continue to operate all other seat units without interruption and without requiring a full system reboot.

Title: Cabin Crew Override
Statement: The cabin crew shall be able to override any passenger-initiated playback and broadcast a safety video or PA announcement to all seat units within 3 seconds of initiating the command.

Title: DO-160G Compliance
Statement: All IFE hardware components installed on the aircraft shall comply with RTCA DO-160G environmental qualification requirements for the applicable installation category.

Title: Passenger Data Privacy
Statement: The IFE system shall not retain any personally identifiable passenger data beyond the end of the flight sector. All session data shall be purged from seat unit volatile memory upon power-down.

Title: Multilingual UI
Statement: The IFE passenger interface shall support a minimum of 10 languages, including the flight origin and destination country languages, switchable at the seat unit level.

Title: Power Budget
Statement: Total IFE system power consumption shall not exceed 8 kW at peak load across a fully-populated wide-body aircraft cabin configuration.

Title: Content Catalogue
Statement: The Applications Server shall maintain a content catalogue of at least 300 audio-visual titles simultaneously accessible to all seat units without degradation in stream quality.

Title: VOD Resume
Statement: The VOD application shall bookmark the last playback position per passenger session and offer resume functionality upon re-selection of the same title within the same flight sector.

Title: Live TV Streaming
Statement: The Applications Server shall support live satellite TV channel streaming with an end-to-end latency not exceeding 8 seconds from broadcast source to seat display.

Title: Content Update
Statement: The content management system shall support ground-based content loading via airport high-speed link, allowing a full catalogue refresh within a 45-minute turnaround window.

Title: Moving Map Application
Statement: The Applications Server shall provide a moving map application displaying real-time aircraft position, altitude, speed, origin, destination, and ETA, updated at intervals not exceeding 30 seconds.

Title: Duty-Free Shopping
Statement: The IFE applications platform shall host a duty-free retail application allowing passengers to browse, select, and purchase items with confirmation transmitted to cabin crew within 10 seconds.

Title: Games Platform
Statement: The applications server shall support at least 15 interactive games playable on the seat display unit, with no additional GPU hardware beyond the standard SDU processing unit.

Title: Application Isolation
Statement: A crash or hang in any individual IFE application (shopping, games, map) shall not affect VOD, audio, or the cabin crew override functions. Application isolation shall be enforced at the OS process level.

Title: Display Resolution
Statement: The Seat Display Unit screen shall provide a minimum resolution of 1920×1080 pixels with a brightness of at least 450 nits to ensure readability in direct sunlight ingress conditions.

Title: Touch Interface
Statement: The SDU shall provide a capacitive multi-touch interface supporting a minimum of 5 simultaneous touch points, operable with gloved hands (glove thickness up to 0.5 mm).

Title: USB Charging
Statement: Each SDU shall provide at least one USB-A (5W) and one USB-C (18W PD) passenger charging port, independently powered from the seat power circuit.

Title: SDU Weight
Statement: The Seat Display Unit assembly including mounting bracket shall not exceed 1.2 kg installed mass per seat to comply with aircraft weight and balance constraints.

Title: SDU MTBF
Statement: The Seat Display Unit shall have a Mean Time Between Failures of not less than 20,000 flight hours under normal operating conditions.

Title: Headphone Jack
Statement: Each SDU shall provide a 3.5 mm stereo audio output jack compatible with standard passenger headsets, with an output level of -10 dBV at 32 Ohm load.

Title: Screen Dimming
Statement: Passengers shall be able to dim the SDU screen brightness to a minimum of 0 nits (screen off) without powering down the unit, to accommodate night flights.

Title: FAR 25.853 Flammability
Statement: All SDU enclosure materials shall meet FAR 25.853 flammability requirements for materials used in aircraft cabin interiors.

Title: CMU Seat Capacity
Statement: A single Cabin Management Unit shall manage the IFE services for a minimum of 50 Seat Display Units without exceeding a 200 ms command response latency.

Title: CMU Redundancy
Statement: The Cabin Management Unit shall support hot-standby redundancy. Failover to the standby CMU shall complete within 5 seconds with no visible interruption to active passenger sessions.

Title: Seat Call Function
Statement: The CMU shall process passenger seat-call requests and route them to the nearest cabin crew attendant panel within 1 second of button activation.

Title: Reading Light Control
Statement: The CMU shall support individual passenger reading light on/off/dim control from the SDU interface with a maximum actuation delay of 500 ms.

Title: CMU Diagnostics
Statement: The CMU shall continuously monitor the health of all connected SDUs, report fault conditions to the ground maintenance system via ACARS within 60 seconds of detection, and log all events to a non-volatile fault log.

Title: Zone Broadcast
Statement: The CMU shall support independent audio/video broadcast to configurable cabin zones (e.g. Business, Economy front, Economy rear) simultaneously with different content per zone.

Title: Audio Channel Count
Statement: The audio subsystem shall support a minimum of 8 simultaneous independent audio channels per Seat Display Unit, covering movie, music, games, PA, and ambient noise channels.

Title: PA Override Priority
Statement: A PA announcement originating from the cockpit or cabin crew panel shall automatically mute all passenger-initiated audio streams and take full priority within 500 ms on all active seat units.

Title: Audio Codec Support
Statement: The audio processing subsystem shall support decoding of AAC-LC, MP3, AC3, and E-AC3 audio streams without additional external DSP hardware.

Title: Noise Floor
Statement: The audio output noise floor at the SDU headphone jack shall be no greater than -70 dBV (A-weighted) in the absence of an audio signal.

Title: Active Noise Cancellation Support
Statement: The SDU audio output shall be compatible with active noise-cancellation headsets conforming to the 3.5 mm CTIA standard without introducing feedback artefacts.

Title: Seat Electronics Box Mounting
Statement: The Seat Electronics Box shall be installed under the passenger seat cushion structure and shall be accessible for line-maintenance replacement without removal of adjacent seat rows.

Title: SEB Operating Temperature
Statement: The Seat Electronics Box shall operate within specification across an ambient temperature range of -20°C to +70°C to accommodate cargo-hold temperature extremes during ground operations.

Title: SEB Power Conditioning
Statement: The Seat Electronics Box shall accept 115 V AC 400 Hz aircraft power and provide regulated DC outputs (5V, 12V, 28V) to all connected seat peripherals with a transient suppression response time of under 10 ms.

Title: SEB EMI
Statement: The Seat Electronics Box shall comply with DO-160G Section 21 Category B radiated and conducted emission limits to prevent interference with aircraft navigation and communication systems.

Title: SEB Connector Locking
Statement: All external connectors on the Seat Electronics Box shall use locking mechanisms rated for a minimum of 500 mating cycles without degradation in electrical contact resistance.

Title: Network Topology
Statement: The IFE cabin distribution network shall use a switched Ethernet topology with a minimum backbone bandwidth of 1 Gbps and per-seat access bandwidth of at least 100 Mbps.

Title: Network Fault Isolation
Statement: A wiring fault on any single cabin distribution segment shall not propagate loss of service to more than one seat row. Fault isolation shall be automatic and shall not require crew intervention.

Title: Cabling Weight
Statement: Total IFE cabin distribution cabling mass including connectors shall not exceed 2.5 kg per seat row on a wide-body aircraft layout.

Title: Network Security Isolation
Statement: The IFE cabin distribution network shall be physically or cryptographically isolated from aircraft avionics networks in compliance with ARINC 664 Part 5 security domain separation requirements.

Title: Wireless Passenger Network
Statement: The IFE system shall provide 802.11ac Wi-Fi connectivity to passenger personal electronic devices with a minimum aggregate throughput of 500 Mbps across the cabin.

Title: Satellite Uplink
Statement: The cabin distribution network shall interface with the aircraft satellite communications unit to provide internet connectivity, with a minimum guaranteed downlink bandwidth of 25 Mbps during cruise phase.

Title: Map Data Currency
Statement: The moving map application shall use navigational chart data with a maximum age of 28 days, updated automatically during ground content loading operations.

Title: Aircraft Position Source
Statement: The moving map application shall receive aircraft position data from the Aircraft Data Gateway (ADG) via ARINC 429 bus at a minimum update rate of 1 Hz.

Title: Map Zoom Levels
Statement: The moving map shall provide at least 5 discrete zoom levels ranging from global view to regional 200 km radius view, selectable by the passenger.

Title: ETA Display
Statement: The moving map application shall display estimated time of arrival computed from current aircraft speed and great-circle distance to destination, updated every 60 seconds.

Title: Airspace Boundaries
Statement: The moving map shall display FIR/UIR airspace boundary overlays sourced from current AIRAC data.

Title: SDU Frame Rate
Statement: The Seat Display Unit shall render video content at a sustained frame rate of 60 fps for 1080p content and 30 fps for 4K content without dropped frames under normal operating conditions.

Title: Channel Change Latency
Statement: Live TV channel change response time from passenger input to first video frame display shall not exceed 3 seconds.

Title: UI Response Time
Statement: The IFE passenger UI shall respond to any touch input within 100 ms (from touch release to visual feedback) under any concurrent content streaming load.

Title: Concurrent HD Streams
Statement: The Applications Server shall support simultaneous independent 1080p H.265 video streams to all seats on a wide-body aircraft (up to 400 seats) without buffering artefacts.

Title: Storage Capacity
Statement: The Applications Server shall provide a minimum of 8 TB of non-volatile storage for content hosting, expandable to 16 TB without hardware architecture change.

Title: Video Codec Support
Statement: The IFE video pipeline shall support hardware-accelerated decoding of H.264, H.265/HEVC, and AV1 video streams at resolutions up to 4K (3840×2160) at 30 fps.

Title: Safety Video Priority
Statement: The IFE system shall interrupt all passenger-selected content and display the safety demonstration video on all seat units upon command from the cabin crew panel, with a maximum delay of 2 seconds.

Title: Seatbelt Sign
Statement: The IFE system shall receive the fasten-seatbelt signal from the aircraft systems bus and automatically display the seatbelt-on reminder graphic on all seat unit screens within 1 second.

Title: Emergency Lighting Control
Statement: The IFE Cabin Management Unit shall interface with the Emergency Lighting Controller and shall relinquish all cabin lighting control authority immediately upon receiving the EMER signal.

Title: No Avionics Interference
Statement: The IFE system shall not generate electromagnetic emissions that interfere with any avionics system during any phase of flight, as verified by DO-160G Section 20 and Section 21 testing.

Title: Cabin Pressure Data
Statement: The IFE system shall receive cabin altitude data from the aircraft pressurisation controller and display a passenger oxygen mask deployment advisory on all screens if cabin altitude exceeds 14,000 ft.

Title: Software Integrity Level
Statement: IFE software components that interface with aircraft avionics buses (ARINC 429 readers) shall be developed and verified to DAL D in accordance with DO-178C.

Title: AES-256 Content Encryption
Statement: All premium and DRM-protected content stored on the Applications Server shall be encrypted at rest using AES-256-GCM. Decryption keys shall be provisioned per-flight via secure ground link and purged at sector end.

Title: Secure Boot
Statement: All IFE server and seat-unit software shall use a hardware-rooted secure boot chain. Any image with an invalid cryptographic signature shall be rejected and an alert logged before power-on completes.

Title: Passenger Network Firewall
Statement: The IFE system shall enforce a stateful packet inspection firewall between the passenger Wi-Fi network and the IFE content distribution network, blocking all traffic not explicitly permitted by the airline operator policy.

Title: Crew Rest Cabin Blackout
Statement: The IFE system shall support a cockpit-initiated cabin blackout command that extinguishes all SDU screens and reading lights simultaneously within 2 seconds, without affecting cabin emergency exit signs.
